exampleSentencesTranslated: I don't want to talk anymore, you go away.
If you don't like it here, just go away.
When they argued, she told him to go away to calm down.
notes_md:
**đi đi** means “go away” or “just go.” It’s usually said when you want someone to leave, often with annoyance or to end a situation.

This is the verb **đi** (to go) repeated for emphasis. Repeating the verb makes the command stronger and more emotional.  

Tone depends on voice: it can be playful, irritated, or clearly telling someone off. A stronger, ruder version is **biến đi**.
notes:
đi đi means “go away” or “just go.” It’s usually said when you want someone to leave, often with annoyance or to end a situation.

This is the verb đi (to go) repeated for emphasis. Repeating the verb makes the command stronger and more emotional.  

Tone depends on voice: it can be playful, irritated, or clearly telling someone off. A stronger, ruder version is biến đi.
exampleSentences: Anh không muốn nói chuyện nữa, em đi đi.
Nếu em không thích ở đây thì cứ đi đi.
Khi cãi nhau, chị bảo anh ấy đi đi để bình tĩnh lại.
